message:
feat(compat/flags,obs/pool): accept sqlite3_open_v2 ancillary flags; clippy tidy connection_pool

crates/fsqlite/src/compat/flags.rs
----------------------------------
Adds first-class recognition (and, where appropriate, accept-and-ignore
semantics) for the ancillary flags that `sqlite3_open_v2` callers pass
alongside the access-mode bits. Previously the compat layer rejected
any flag bit outside `READ_ONLY | READ_WRITE | CREATE`, which broke
drop-in clients that passed stock SQLite masks such as
`SQLITE_OPEN_READWRITE | SQLITE_OPEN_CREATE | SQLITE_OPEN_URI |
SQLITE_OPEN_NOMUTEX`.

New `OpenFlags` constants:

* `SQLITE_OPEN_URI = 0x40` — URI path interpretation. Accepted for
  parity; URI query-parameter semantics are still a TODO, but the
  compat layer no longer refuses to open the connection.
* `SQLITE_OPEN_NO_MUTEX = 0x0000_8000` and
  `SQLITE_OPEN_FULL_MUTEX = 0x0001_0000` — connection-mutex mode
  selectors. FrankenSQLite does not expose SQLite's per-connection
  mutex toggling, so both are accepted and ignored.
* `SQLITE_OPEN_SHARED_CACHE = 0x0002_0000` and
  `SQLITE_OPEN_PRIVATE_CACHE = 0x0004_0000` — shared-cache
  participation. FrankenSQLite does not implement SQLite's
  shared-cache subsystem, so again accepted and ignored.
* `SQLITE_OPEN_EXRESCODE = 0x0200_0000` — extended result codes.
  FrankenSQLite already returns rich Rust error variants; the flag
  is accepted and ignored.

New crate-private masks:

* `ACCESS_MODE_MASK` — the union of the three access-mode bits.
* `ACCEPTED_ANCILLARY_MASK` — the union of the six new ancillary
  bits above.
* `SUPPORTED_MASK` — the union of both, used to reject unknown bits.

`classify_access_mode` now runs a new `validate_open_flags(flags)`
pre-check and then masks the incoming flags against
`ACCESS_MODE_MASK` before doing its existing read-only/read-write/
create decision. `validate_open_flags` enforces three invariants
and returns a structured `FrankenError::TypeMismatch` if any of
them is violated:

1. Any bit outside `SUPPORTED_MASK` is rejected with
   `"unsupported open flag bits 0x<hex>"`. This catches both typos
   and features we genuinely do not implement.
2. Setting both `SQLITE_OPEN_NO_MUTEX` and `SQLITE_OPEN_FULL_MUTEX`
   is rejected as a conflicting configuration.
3. Setting both `SQLITE_OPEN_SHARED_CACHE` and
   `SQLITE_OPEN_PRIVATE_CACHE` is rejected as a conflicting
   configuration.

New tests
---------
* `classify_access_mode_accepts_common_sqlite_ancillary_flags` — the
  exact `READ_WRITE | CREATE | URI | NO_MUTEX | PRIVATE_CACHE | EXRESCODE`
  mask used by real clients returns `OpenDisposition::WriteCreate`.
* `classify_access_mode_rejects_conflicting_mutex_flags` — passing
  both `NO_MUTEX` and `FULL_MUTEX` yields `FrankenError::TypeMismatch`.
* `classify_access_mode_rejects_conflicting_cache_flags` — passing
  both `SHARED_CACHE` and `PRIVATE_CACHE` yields
  `FrankenError::TypeMismatch`.

crates/fsqlite-observability/src/connection_pool.rs
---------------------------------------------------
Clippy tidy — no behaviour changes:

* Derives `Eq` on `ConnectionPoolRecommendation` alongside the
  existing `PartialEq` so downstream consumers can stick it in
  hash-based containers.
* `recommended_pool_size`, `write_parallelism_target`, and
  `detect_single_connection_serialization` are promoted to associated
  functions (no `&self` receiver) because they do not read any
  validator config. Call sites updated to
  `Self::recommended_pool_size(sample)` / `Self::write_parallelism_target(sample)`
  / `Self::detect_single_connection_serialization(sample, &mut findings)`.
* `write_parallelism_target`'s `ReadHeavy` arm switches from
  `.max(1).min(2)` to the clearer `observed_need.clamp(1, 2)`, and
  the identical `Mixed` / `WriteHeavy` arms are merged with an
  `|` pattern so we no longer repeat `observed_need.max(2)` on two
  separate lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
